title: Slices in Rust
content:
### Slices in Rust

A slice is a reference to a contiguous sequence of elements in a collection. Slices are fat pointers: they store a pointer and a length.

---

### Slice Types

| Type | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `&[T]` | Slice of elements of type T |
| `&str` | String slice (special) |
| `&mut [T]` | Mutable slice |

---

### Working with Slices

```rust
let arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];

let slice = &arr[1..3];     // elements at index 1, 2
let full = &arr[..];         // all elements
let from = &arr[2..];        // from index 2 to end
let to = &arr[..3];          // from start to index 2

// String slices
let s = String::from("hello world");
let hello = &s[0..5];       // "hello"
let world = &s[6..11];      // "world"
let whole = &s[..];           // "hello world"

// Functions accepting slices
fn first_word(s: &str) -> &str {
    &s[..s.find(' ').unwrap_or(s.len())]
}
```

> **Key Point:** String slice indices operate on bytes, not characters. Slicing at a non-UTF-8 boundary will panic. Prefer using string methods like `find`, `split`, or `chars` for safe string manipulation.

quiz.question: What does a slice reference store?
